label: GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest

environment.caveat: virtualized CI runner: shared vCPUs, noisy neighbours, no real-time scheduling -- absolute latency (esp. tails) is worse and noisier than bare metal; use for cross-platform coverage and relative comparison.

uncontrolled_noise: no isolated cores (isolcpus=) -> scheduler + IRQ noise

results.name: ring put+get, amortized 1-core
results.note: pure ring op cost; clock CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW res 1.0 ns

results.name: cross-thread one-way latency
results.note: 9.3% <100 ns; max at 55% of run (mid-run scheduler jitter); no RT

results.name: cross-process one-way latency
results.note: 9.3% <100 ns; max at 87% of run (mid-run scheduler jitter); no RT

results.name: ring put+get, amortized 1-core (RT)
results.note: pure ring op cost; clock CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW res 1.0 ns

results.name: cross-thread one-way latency (RT)
results.note: 50.2% <100 ns; max at 21% of run (mid-run scheduler jitter); RT hardened

results.name: cross-process one-way latency (RT)
results.note: 39.3% <100 ns; max at 74% of run (mid-run scheduler jitter); RT hardened

results.note: ECDH P-256 + ML-KEM encaps/decaps + HKDF-SHA384 combine, both parties

results.name: ECDH-P256+ML-KEM-1024
results.note: ECDH P-256 + ML-KEM encaps/decaps + HKDF-SHA384 combine, both parties

results.name: NIST-1 (ML-KEM-512 + ML-DSA-44)
results.note: 3 sign + 3 verify + ML-KEM + 2 ECDH + 2 HKDF, both parties; identities provisioned out-of-band (not timed); isolated from DDS transport

results.name: NIST-3 (ML-KEM-768 + ML-DSA-65)
results.note: 3 sign + 3 verify + ML-KEM + 2 ECDH + 2 HKDF, both parties; identities provisioned out-of-band (not timed); isolated from DDS transport

results.name: CNSA 2.0 / NIST-5 (ML-KEM-1024 + ML-DSA-87)
results.note: 3 sign + 3 verify + ML-KEM + 2 ECDH + 2 HKDF, both parties; identities provisioned out-of-band (not timed); isolated from DDS transport

results.name: key agreement only — hybrid ECDH+ML-KEM rekey (no identity sig)
results.note: the recurring/rekey cost is the hybrid_kem group: ~128 µs (ECDH+ML-KEM+HKDF, no signatures)

results.name: CycloneDDS
results.note: CycloneDDS PQC test binary not built on this platform

results.name: FastDDS
results.note: Fast DDS PKI-DH test binary not built on this platform

results.name: swapover — Apple M5 (unified memory) (modeled)
results.note: projected dispatch+sync = 204 us. CALIBRATED: GPU saturates ~120 GB/s unified LPDDR5; numpy CPU ~17.5 GB/s effective -> reproduces the measured ~4 MiB crossover

results.name: crossover batch — Apple M5 (unified memory) (modeled)
results.note: GPU overtakes CPU above ~4.0 MiB resident / 4.0 MiB cold (host<->device copy each batch); ~4,081 concurrent 1-KiB streams. Roofline projection — see GPGPU-Sim/Accel-Sim for cycle-accurate. CALIBRATED: GPU saturates ~120 GB/s unified LPDDR5; numpy CPU ~17.5 GB/s effective -> reproduces the measured ~4 MiB crossover

results.name: swapover — NVIDIA A100 80GB (PCIe4 x16) (modeled)
results.note: projected dispatch+sync = 8 us. PROJECTED: HBM2e 1.94 TB/s, PCIe4 x16 ~25 GB/s effective, ~8 us launch

results.name: crossover batch — NVIDIA A100 80GB (PCIe4 x16) (modeled)
results.note: GPU overtakes CPU above ~319 KiB resident / never (per-byte slower) cold (host<->device copy each batch); ~319 concurrent 1-KiB streams. Roofline projection — see GPGPU-Sim/Accel-Sim for cycle-accurate. PROJECTED: HBM2e 1.94 TB/s, PCIe4 x16 ~25 GB/s effective, ~8 us launch

results.name: swapover — NVIDIA H100 (PCIe5 x16) (modeled)
results.note: projected dispatch+sync = 6 us. PROJECTED: HBM3 3.35 TB/s, PCIe5 x16 ~55 GB/s effective, ~6 us launch

results.name: crossover batch — NVIDIA H100 (PCIe5 x16) (modeled)
results.note: GPU overtakes CPU above ~237 KiB resident / never (per-byte slower) cold (host<->device copy each batch); ~237 concurrent 1-KiB streams. Roofline projection — see GPGPU-Sim/Accel-Sim for cycle-accurate. PROJECTED: HBM3 3.35 TB/s, PCIe5 x16 ~55 GB/s effective, ~6 us launch

results.name: swapover — NVIDIA H100 (NVLink) (modeled)
results.note: projected dispatch+sync = 6 us. PROJECTED: HBM3 3.35 TB/s, NVLink ~450 GB/s effective, ~6 us launch

results.name: crossover batch — NVIDIA H100 (NVLink) (modeled)
results.note: GPU overtakes CPU above ~237 KiB resident / 289 KiB cold (host<->device copy each batch); ~237 concurrent 1-KiB streams. Roofline projection — see GPGPU-Sim/Accel-Sim for cycle-accurate. PROJECTED: HBM3 3.35 TB/s, NVLink ~450 GB/s effective, ~6 us launch

results.name: swapover — AMD MI250 (PCIe4 x16) (modeled)
results.note: projected dispatch+sync = 8 us. PROJECTED: HBM2e ~3.2 TB/s (per-GCD aggregate), PCIe4 x16 ~25 GB/s, ~8 us launch

results.name: crossover batch — AMD MI250 (PCIe4 x16) (modeled)
results.note: GPU overtakes CPU above ~316 KiB resident / never (per-byte slower) cold (host<->device copy each batch); ~316 concurrent 1-KiB streams. Roofline projection — see GPGPU-Sim/Accel-Sim for cycle-accurate. PROJECTED: HBM2e ~3.2 TB/s (per-GCD aggregate), PCIe4 x16 ~25 GB/s, ~8 us launch

note: Measured on this host only. Numbers are platform/CPU/build-specific. Re-run the identical script on each target OS to compare. Skipped rows indicate a capability/dependency absent on THIS platform, not a failure.
